name: The Farmers State Bank
description_short: Community bank founded in 1903 offering FDIC-insured checking, savings, CDs, and loan products with a focus on agricultural and local lending.
description_long: Farmers State Bank was established on May 3, 1903, by L.A. Libel and 39 citizens who each contributed $10,000 to launch the institution in Wathena, Kansas. The bank has operated continuously for over 120 years, surviving the Great Depression, two World Wars, multiple natural disasters, and two bank robberies while maintaining its original founding principles of community service and sound banking. The bank is FDIC-insured and backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, providing depositors with federal protection up to applicable limits. The bank offers a comprehensive suite of financial services including checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), vehicle loans, agricultural loans, real estate loans, and commercial loans. Customers can access online banking with bill pay capabilities, eStatements, check imaging, and check reordering. The bank also issues a Farmers State Bank Platinum Visa Credit Card and provides financial calculators for loans, mortgages, retirement, and savings planning. Farmers State Bank distinguishes itself through its deep community roots and specialization in agricultural lending, reflecting its heritage in a farming region. The bank has expanded and modernized over the decades—most recently with a major expansion and remodeling in 1993—while maintaining its local, relationship-based banking approach. Leadership has remained within the founding Libel family through multiple generations, with L.A. Libel, III assuming the presidency at age 25 in 1967. As a smaller community bank, Farmers State Bank offers personalized service and agricultural expertise but may have limited product innovation compared to larger national institutions. The website provides basic online banking tools and current rates (savings at 0.10%, 15-month CD at 0.40%, 36-month CD at 0.60%, 48-month CD at 0.70%), though rates are relatively modest. The bank appears well-capitalized and stable with over a century of uninterrupted operation, making it a reliable choice for customers seeking relationship banking in rural Kansas.
services: Farmers State Bank Platinum Visa Credit Card
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by U.S. Government guarantee
Over 120 years of continuous operation and stability since 1903
Specialized agricultural lending expertise suited to farming customers
Online banking with bill pay, eStatements, and check imaging capabilities
Local relationship banking with family leadership continuity
Recently expanded facility (1993 remodeling added 2,500 sq ft)
Free annual credit report access for customers
cons: Very low interest rates on savings accounts (0.10%) and CDs compared to online banks
Limited geographic footprint as small rural bank may restrict convenience for non-local customers
Website appears dated with basic functionality; limited digital innovation compared to larger competitors
Small institution may have fewer advanced financial products or investment services
No mention of mobile banking app or modern digital-first features
best_for: Agricultural customers needing farm loans and agribusiness financing
Rural Kansas residents seeking relationship banking with local decision-making
Depositors prioritizing stability and FDIC insurance over maximum yield
Small business owners in farming and agricultural sectors
diagnosis: Farmers State Bank is best for rural Kansas residents and agricultural customers seeking stable, FDIC-insured banking with agricultural lending expertise and relationship-based service. The main caveat is that deposit rates are significantly lower than online-only banks, and the institution's limited geographic footprint and modest digital capabilities make it less suitable for customers prioritizing competitive yields or advanced fintech features.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
